reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real ~636MB download + on-device WASM inference of ViT-Huge/14, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — model page + all 4 use-case pages

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — REAL WASM run: fresh Chrome profile → 'Download model (~636 MB)' → click → the full 636MB q8 ViT-Huge/14 ONNX downloaded, auto-init → 'Model ready', then onnx-community/ijepa_vith14_1k produced last_hidden_state [1,256,1280] entirely in a Web Worker. Node cross-check confirmed the same shape (256 patch tokens on a 16×16 grid, hidden 1280, no [CLS]). conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. Screens: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-shots/ijepa-features-desktop.png, -mobile.png.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified in-browser; 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Heavy 636MB download completed and ran in a tab.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real 6×6 cosine-similarity matrix (36 cells) with the diagonal all 1.00 (self-similarity) and real off-diagonal values from genuine 1280-d mean-pooled descriptors — not a canned or degenerate all-ones matrix. Readout: dim=1280, patches=256, backend=WASM. io-shape-semantic=manual. Screens: desktop.png.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified: real embeddings, diagonal=1.00, off-diagonal computed.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 5✓ — declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/ijepa_vith14_1k linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-feature-extraction), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8 → onnx/model_quantized.onnx), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker). At-a-glance grounded in the real I-JEPA model card + config (ViT-Huge/14, 256 patches, hidden 1280, no [CLS]/registers, ImageNet-1k). License declared honestly as CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial).
rubric.notes: Distinct predictive-SSL objective (predict representations of masked regions) documented, not conflated with DINOv2/CLIP.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~636 MB)' (state=download-required); after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'Model ready' (state=ready) with a 'Clear cached model' control + storage detail. The large model is NEVER auto-downloaded — Download is user-initiated only (invariant 12). conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified auto-init path; heavy 636MB model gated behind explicit Download.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — download progress streamed on the loader; every page routes load/inference failures to a .status.err region; a Retry button surfaces on failed init (model-loader.js). Runs on the WASM path (no GPU). A constrained device that runs out of memory on the 636MB ViT-Huge surfaces the failure on the page with Retry — never a faked result. conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline=manual, runs-without-webgpu-fallback=manual.
rubric.notes: Heavy-download honesty: exact size + 'best on desktop' note shown up front.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real, labelled, keyboard-operable controls across pages: image tray + dropzone (Enter/Space) + Embed&compare + Reset + mode toggles + inspect select (main); preset chips + file inputs + mode toggles + Compare (basics); tray + dropzone + threshold slider + query select (practical); preset chips + file inputs + confidence slider + Match (wild); two loaders + query select + upload + Retrieve&classify (multi-model). No dead controls. conformance: buttons-have-names=pass; controls-operable=manual.
rubric.notes: Threshold slider re-colours correspondence from cached patch embeddings — no re-inference.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — every number/visual is computed from the worker's real embeddings: the 6×6 matrix (diagonal 1.00), the patch cosine/norm heatmaps, the near-duplicate + reverse-search rankings, the dense correspondence field, and the multi-model retrieve→ViT-classify chain. No canned output anywhere. no-fake-output=manual.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real output on the model page; use-case pages share the same real engine.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Patch heatmaps carry role=img + aria-label; the similarity matrix uses th scope=col/row with thumbnail alts; status is role=status aria-live; the dropzone is role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space handling. Guidance 'accessibility' + 'expose-canvas-content-to-browser-features' applied.
rubric.notes: Semantic table + labelled grids + live regions + keyboard dropzone.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 driven: measured documentElement.scrollWidth−clientWidth = 0px at mobile; grid-2/corr-pair use auto-fit minmax and collapse to one column; wide matrix sits in an overflow-x:auto scroll container; images are inline-size:100% max. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. support=ok/ok with an honest heavy-download caveat for constrained mobile.

Agent verdict — ALL inference runs off the main thread in a module Web Worker; the mean-pool + per-patch cosine/norm loops and the dense correspondence matching stay in the worker (patch embeddings transferred, not cloned). The interaction path only posts a message and awaits a result — no blocking loop. 0 console errors during the 6-image matrix embed. Guidance 'break-up-long-tasks' applied. inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: ViT-Huge is compute-heavy, but the main thread stays free because inference is off-thread.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout with flex-wrap+gap, repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) grids, min-inline-size:0 on long content, and wide content in overflow-x:auto so the body never scrolls horizontally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grid-2 / corr-pair use auto-fit minmax(min(280px,100%),1fr); the similarity matrix sits in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); the image tray + toolbars use flex-wrap+gap; logical properties (inline-size/block-size) throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: mobile 360×740 measured 0px horizontal overflow on the model page (responsive-desktop/mobile conformance = pass).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy synchronous work off the main thread / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model inference AND the per-patch cosine/norm + mean-pool + dense-correspondence maths run in a module Web Worker; unit patch embeddings are transferred back. No main-thread inference loop exists to break up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js posts results; 0 console errors during headless embed.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose visual/grid content to assistive tech with role=img + a text alternative.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the patch heatmaps and correspondence fields are role=img grids with descriptive aria-labels; the similarity matrix is a real semantic <table> with thumbnail alts rather than a canvas.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance images-have-alt=pass; grids labelled in index.html/use-case pages.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control, announce state in a live region, use semantic tables (th scope), keyboard-operate custom widgets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live loader/status; mode chips use aria-pressed; the dropzone is role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space; the matrix uses th scope=col/row.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); pages link the shared stylesheet.

openQuestions: onnx-community also ships ijepa_vith16_1k / ijepa_vith14_22k — worth a separate demo to contrast patch size (14 vs 16) or pretraining scale (1k vs 22k)? Additive, not a replacement. All are ViT-Huge (~600MB+), so a size warning would be essential.
The CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial) license limits real deployment — is the current at-a-glance note prominent enough, or should the catalogue blurb lead with it?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a smaller/faster image-feature backbone comparison so users on constrained connections have a lightweight predictive-vs-contrastive option alongside the 636MB I-JEPA — additive.

Add a side-by-side page that runs I-JEPA + DINOv2-with-registers on the same image and contrasts their patch-norm / cosine maps (predictive vs self-distillation SSL) — a NEW multi-model page, additive.

summary: I-JEPA (predictive self-supervised image features) adds a DISTINCT pretraining objective to the showcase's vision-embedding family: prediction IN REPRESENTATION SPACE (predict the encoder's representations of masked regions from a context block) — no pixel reconstruction (MAE), no image-image contrast (DINO/CLIP), no [CLS] or register tokens. Same image→descriptor capability as DINOv2/CLIP, grounded honestly in I-JEPA's own objective. Verified end-to-end on real hardware — fresh profile → Download (~636MB q8 ViT-Huge/14) → auto-init → run, real last_hidden_state [1,256,1280], a genuine 6×6 cosine matrix (diagonal 1.00), plus near-dup/reverse-search, dense correspondence, and a retrieve→ViT-classify chain. All inference off the main thread. 0 console errors, 0px mobile overflow; license declared as CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial) and the 636MB size surfaced up front with honest degradation. conformance 14/14 auto-pass + 9 agent-evidenced manual.
